message:
fix(io/read_ext): rollback read_to_string buffer on UTF-8 errors

ReadToString previously left partially-decoded valid bytes appended to
the caller's String buffer when it later encountered invalid or
incomplete UTF-8.  This meant the caller saw corrupted state after a
failed read_to_string call — the buffer would contain stale partial
data that wasn't there before the call.

Fix: track the initial buffer length at construction time and truncate
back to it on any UTF-8 error (both invalid byte sequences and
incomplete trailing multibyte sequences).

- Add `start_len` field to ReadToString to snapshot pre-call state
- Add `rollback_utf8_error()` method that truncates buf and clears
  the pending_utf8 accumulator
- Call rollback on both the incomplete-sequence (EOF with trailing bytes)
  and invalid-byte (push_valid_prefix failure) error paths
- New test: read_to_string_invalid_utf8_rolls_back_after_long_valid_prefix
  verifies a 1024-byte valid prefix followed by 0xFF leaves the buffer
  at its "seed" value, not "seed" + 1024 'a' chars
- New test: read_to_string_incomplete_utf8_rolls_back_after_long_valid_prefix
  verifies a 1024-byte valid prefix followed by a truncated 4-byte emoji
  sequence (3 of 4 bytes) also rolls back cleanly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
